# Design: {{name}}

## Architecture Overview

> **Definition:** High-level system design, components, and interactions.  
> **Rule:** Must be understandable without implementation details; use diagrams.

## Interfaces

> **Definition:** External or internal endpoints, contracts, and protocols.  
> **Rule:** Avoid assumptions; every contract must be documented.

## Logic

> **Definition:** Core logic or processing required to implement the feature.  
> **Rule:** Must directly map to functional requirements in the PRD.

## Edge Cases & Constraints

> **Definition:** Special cases, limits, or environmental considerations.  
> **Rule:** Must include anything that could break the implementation if
> ignored.

## Test Plan

> **Definition:** How the feature will be tested and validated.  
> **Rule:** Must include unit, integration, and acceptance tests tied to PRD
> success criteria.

## Documentation Changes

> **Definition:** Updates needed for README.md files.
> **Rule:** Must cover setup, usage, and any new concepts introduced.

## Development Environment Changes

> **Definition:** Modifications to Brewfile, setup scripts, environment
> variables, etc.
> **Rule:** Must ensure every new engineer can onboard smoothly.

## Tasks

> **Definition:** Breakdown of the design into bite-sized, actionable,
> step-by-step tasks.  
> **Rule:** Each task's scope must be clearly defined with strict requirements
> and success criteria.
